description: Lifecycle case (incident response) with a reversed decision and a ruled-out/deprecated item; the chat contains the superseded content, the live state does not.

chat.content: Checkout is failing for ~20% of users since 14:00. First guess?
Likely a network/DNS issue between the gateway and the payment service. Investigate there first.
Network looks clean end to end. Latency normal.
Then the network hypothesis is ruled out. The real cause is an expired TLS certificate on the payment service — handshakes are failing.
Should we roll back the 13:30 deploy?
Rollback won't help — the cert expiry is independent of the deploy.
Correct, reversing that: do NOT roll back. The decision is to forward-fix by rotating the certificate.
Any constraint on how we fix it?
No customer-facing downtime during business hours — the rotation must be hot.
Comms: support wants a public status-page post now; PR wants to wait until it's resolved. Open.
Who owns the postmortem — undecided. So: cause is the expired TLS cert, fix is hot cert rotation (no rollback), no business-hours downtime, comms timing and postmortem owner open.
